name: zoey bethel
ai_summary: Zoey Bethel from Augusta, Georgia is a certified pediatric medical assistant with CPR, first aid, and medical assistant certifications, experienced with children from newborns to young adults. She is available part-time around her college schedule and uses distraction and positive reinforcement to resolve conflicts.

transcripts.q3:
Hi, my name is Zoe and I am a pediatric medical assistant in North Augusta. I work with kids from newborns around, two days, old to 22 year olds. I administer vaccines and medication. I also have a CPR PLS and first aid, certification that is active for the next two years, as well as a certified medical assistant certification from the NHA. And I'm just applying so I can have more our flexibility.
 T I love working with kids, it's so fresh and refreshing way more than adults and I'm very excited to be able to do that more.
transcripts.q4: I'm currently located in Augusta, Georgia and the central area near the Forest Hills campus, and I'm willing to locate up to 45 minutes for work.
transcripts.q5: I'm currently in college so I'm looking for part-time work. I can work on the weekends on any shifts but I need at least three weekdays two to three week days off so I can attend classes.
transcripts.q6:
When I am working as a medical assistant, I have a lot of appointments that are siblings or triplets and it can get pretty Rowdy, especially when we start to bring the shots out or something happens that they're not too happy about. And the best way that I've handled the situations is just making sure that the kids are distracted and understand that those behaviors are not going to be rewarded and then rewarding those behaviors that are good when they start to.
 To get along. Give the other kid a hug things like that.
transcripts.q7:
I think I would make an amazing kid teacher, because, not only do I have CPR certifications. That would allow me to give emergency care if needed, which I think is probably one of the most important aspects to leaving your kid alone with an adult. And also I have experience dealing with kids and talking to them during scary situations that they might not be super comfortable with and understanding how to handle the situations. And I feel like a lot, a big part of my
 My job as a healthcare worker, is to get a kid to cooperate with me, which would be a big task for a teacher. So, I feel like I'm equipped with that and I understand how to deal with that.
